predictions.rationale: Market backs France at ~65% in the toughest group of the draw. France name a full-strength XI — Mbappé at the 9 with Olise and Dembélé behind, and Saliba/Koundé both shaking off niggles to start. Senegal carry genuine threat through Sadio Mané, so this is no cakewalk; France's quality should tell but a draw-heavy tournament so far keeps confidence moderate.

predictions.rationale: Norway are overwhelming ~79% favourites, carrying Haaland's firepower into a group they must mine for points behind France. Iraq's ceiling is a low block and a smash-and-grab. Backing Norway away with a clean, controlled margin.

sources.title: World Cup 2026 Group I guide – Sky Sports

predictions.rationale: Holders Argentina open at ~67% and are Opta's clear group favourite (~72%). Messi-led quality and tournament pedigree against an Algeria side that must defend deep. Comfortable but not flawless — Argentina rarely run up big margins early.

sources.title: World Cup 2026 winners predicted – Sky Sports

predictions.rationale: Austria are ~70% favourites, the clear second seed in Group J and a level above tournament debutants Jordan. Their pressing, set-piece threat and squad depth should carry the day; Jordan's hope is to stay compact and frustrate.

predictions.rationale: Portugal sit at ~74% with a deep, talented squad and a settled spine. DR Congo are athletic and awkward but a clear tier below. Expect Portugal to control possession and win comfortably without necessarily being prolific.

sources.title: 2026 World Cup group previews – RotoWire

predictions.rationale: England are favoured at ~55% and are Opta's third-likeliest overall group winner, but Croatia's veteran midfield control makes this the group's marquee test. England's superior attacking depth should edge it; the draw price (26%) is live given Croatia's tournament know-how.

predictions.rationale: Close to a pick'em — Ghana lead at just 43% with the draw and Panama near level. Ghana's pace and individual quality give a slight edge, but Panama are organised and dangerous on transition. Low confidence on a tight, low-scoring game.

predictions.rationale: Czechia are modest ~54% favourites with more individual quality and a need to chase Group A leaders. South Africa are quick and well-drilled and can frustrate. Lean Czech but in a tight, potentially low-scoring contest.

predictions.rationale: Brazil at ~87% is the strongest single-match favourite on the slate, and Neymar is targeting this fixture to feature. Haiti are massively outclassed. The only question is the margin — backing a comfortable Brazil rout.

predictions.disagreement: Sweden's emphatic opener makes the away side more dangerous than the market's 19% suggests.
